panels.description: Wide shot: A dark, antique wooden bottle with a rolled parchment inside, bobbing slightly on a vast, choppy grey sea under a stormy sky. Overlayed narration boxes detailing the captain's final message and the verdict regarding the murders.

bubbles.text: If we are wrecked, mayhap this bottle may be found, and those who find it may understand... God help a poor soul doing his duty.
The verdict was open. No evidence. The folk here hold the captain is a hero, to be given a public funeral.

panels.description: Wide shot: A solemn procession of numerous small fishing boats trailing behind a larger vessel on dark water, moving towards a distant, rugged cliffside abbey. The mood is respectful but somber.

bubbles.text: His body is to be taken up the Esk for a piece, then back to the pier for burial on the cliff. Over a hundred boats will follow him.
No trace of the great dog has ever been found; much mourning ensues.

panels.description: Medium close-up: Jonathan stands alone, looking out towards a dark, restless sea at night. His face shows exhaustion and deep worry under a low-hanging moon. The sound of the storm is implied.

bubbles.text: To-morrow will see the funeral; and so will end this one more 'mystery of the sea.'
Lucy was very restless all night. The storm was fearful... each sharp puff seemed like a distant gun.

panels.description: Close-up on Mina Murray's face, asleep but deeply troubled, illuminated by pale moonlight filtering into a dark bedroom. Jonathan's hand is visible near her shoulder, gently trying to guide her.

bubbles.text: Fortunately, I managed to undress her without waking her.
Strangely enough, Lucy got up twice and dressed herself... but did not wake.

panels.description: Medium shot: Mina sits by a window, holding her head, looking deeply troubled by Lucy's behavior. Soft morning light suggests an internal conflict.

bubbles.text: As soon as her will is thwarted physically, she yields almost exactly to the routine of her life.

panels.description: Wide shot: Morning at the harbour. Bright sunlight contrasts sharply with dark, turbulent waves crashing into the narrow mouth of the port. A few weary figures stand near the edge.

bubbles.text: Early in the morning we both got up and went down to the harbour to see if anything had happened.
The waves, topped with snow-like foam, forced themselves in like a bullying man through a crowd.

panels.description: Medium close-up montage style panel: Focus is tight on Mina's anguished expression, eyes wide with worry, looking off-panel towards the sea. The background is blurred by sea spray and oppressive sunlight.

bubbles.text: I felt glad Jonathan was on land... But where is he now? On land or sea?
I am getting fearfully anxious! If only I knew what to do!
The funeral of the poor sea-captain today was most touching.
